title: trisetaria distichophylla
intro: Trisetaria distichophylla, also known as Red Fescue, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions. Trisetaria distichophylla has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as acting as a natural diuretic to help with urinary issues. Herbal preparations of Trisetaria distichophylla can be made by infusing the plant in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding it to make a powder for use in tinctures or capsules.
